name: Plasma rejuvenation review reinforces clinical translation boundaries
summary: Review-level context supports separating plasma-based rejuvenation strategies by modality, endpoint, and translational maturity rather than treating them as one intervention class.
statement: A 2026 review of plasma-based systemic rejuvenation supports cautious clinical translation and keeps plasma dilution, young plasma, TPE, donor-product exposure, and factor-specific mechanisms separated.
population_or_model: Review-level synthesis across plasma-based systemic rejuvenation literature
caveats: Review context is not new primary efficacy evidence.
The record supports taxonomy and interpretation boundaries rather than a stage upgrade.
Primary records remain necessary for any future rating change.
